title: Payment Gateways for Cameroon Digital Products: My Unfiltered Take After Helping Hundreds of Creators
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: I've seen countless digital creators in Cameroon struggle to sell their ebooks, courses, and software products internationally due to the limitations imposed by PayPal, Stripe, and other payment gateways. The reality is harsh: these creators are forced to choose between abandoning their dreams or giving up a significant portion of their earnings to intermediaries. In this post, I'll share my unfiltered take on the payment gateways for Cameroon digital products, drawing from my experience helping hundreds of creators overcome these obstacles.
The Honest Reality Most Creators Avoid

Most creators in Cameroon are unaware of the true cost of using popular payment gateways like PayPal and Stripe. Not only do these platforms charge high fees, but they also impose unnecessary restrictions on who can use them, essentially blocking creators from reaching their global audience. This is not a minor issue; it's a major roadblock to success, and one that I've seen many creators try to navigate in vain.
Why the Popular Advice Is Wrong

The conventional wisdom is that creators need to find alternative payment methods or work with local banks to accept payments. While this might seem like a viable solution, it's often a dead-end street. Local banks can be unreliable, and alternative payment methods often come with their own set of fees and restrictions. The truth is, there's a better way to sell digital products internationally without breaking the bank or sacrificing your creative freedom.
What the Data and Experience Actually Show

My experience working with hundreds of creators in Cameroon and other restricted countries has shown me that there's a clear demand for a more flexible and cost-effective payment solution. With the right tools and platforms, creators can reach a global audience without sacrificing their earnings or autonomy. This is exactly what our Web3 Wallet Connect product ($19.99) offers: a frontend wallet integration with multi-chain QR generation and live price feeds, allowing buyers to pay in BTC, ETH, USDT, SOL, and 300 more coins.
Blockchain-based payments are the future of international digital sales for creators in Cameroon.
The Uncomfortable Truth About Your Options

Let's face it: the status quo is unacceptable. Creators in Cameroon deserve better than to be limited by outdated payment gateways and restrictive platforms. The truth is, there are alternative solutions available, and they're not as complicated or expensive as you might think. It's time to take control of your sales and earnings, and to stop letting intermediaries dictate your success.
What You Should Do Based on All of This
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

The conversion typically takes 20-30 minutes and costs 2-5% of the transaction, which is significantly less than PayPal's international transfer fees or Western Union's rates. This is the complete payment flow that thousands of creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are using right now.
The days of sacrificing earnings to intermediaries are numbered for creators in restricted countries like Cameroon.
